title: Aluminum Supply Shock — Gulf Smelters Stranded by Hormuz Closure
close_reason: Superseded by `aluminum-supply-shock` (High Conviction, AA + CENX) — same lead ticker, same Hormuz/strikes mechanism, overlapping 3-6 vs 6-12 month timeframe. Closing to honor the concentration limit and consolidate the AA exposure under one active opp.
what_worked: Identified the second-order aluminum exposure to Hormuz before the broader market caught up; the brownfield data center monetization angle was a genuinely differentiated catalyst.
what_didnt: Filed as a separate radar item when an active High Conviction opp already covered the AA Hormuz thesis — duplicative shelf-space we didn't need. Entry price (58.41) is approximated from key_metrics at filing since no formal trade_construction was recorded.
would_have_done: Should have amended `aluminum-supply-shock` to reference the brownfield monetization catalyst inside its existing thesis rather than opening a parallel opp.
